title: Which preferences associate with school performance?—Lessons from an exploratory study with university students
abstract: Success in life is determined to a large extent by school performance so it is important to understand the effect of the factors that influence it. In this exploratory study, in addition to cognitive abilities, we attempt to link measures of preferences with outcomes of school performance. We measured in an incentivized way risk, time, social and competitive preferences and cognitive abilities of university students to look for associations between these measures and two important academic outcome measures: exam results and GPA. We find consistently that cognitive abilities (proxied by the Cognitive Reflection Test) are very well correlated with school performance. Regarding non-cognitive skills, we report suggestive evidence for many of our measured preferences. We used two alternative measures of time preference: patience and present bias. Present bias explains exam grades better, while patience explains GPA relatively better. Both measures of time preferences have a non-linear relation to school performance. Competitiveness matters, as students, who opt for a more competitive payment scheme in our experimental task have a higher average GPA. We observe also that risk-averse students perform a little better than more risk-tolerant students. That makes sense in case of multiple choice exams, because more risk-tolerant students may want to try to pass the exam less prepared, as the possibility of passing an exam just by chance is not zero. Finally, we have also detected that cooperative preferences-the amount of money offered in a public good game-associates strongly with GPA in a non-linear way. Students who offered around half of their possible amounts had significantly higher GPAs than those, who offered none or all their money.
summary_en: Do patient, risk-taking, or competitive students do better in school? This exploratory paper measures risk, time, social, and competitive preferences in Hungarian university students with incentivized experimental tasks, and links them to exam grades and GPA. Cognitive abilities (proxied by the Cognitive Reflection Test) correlate strongly with performance; among non-cognitive measures, both patience and present bias matter but in different ways — present bias better predicts exam grades, while patience is more strongly linked to GPA. A building-block paper for thinking about which preferences matter for human-capital formation.
summary_hu: A türelmes, kockázatvállaló vagy versengő diákok teljesítenek-e jobban az iskolában? Ez a feltáró tanulmány magyar egyetemistáknál méri a kockázati, idő-, szociális és versengési preferenciákat ösztönzött kísérleti feladatokkal, és összekapcsolja ezeket a vizsgajegyekkel és átlagokkal. A kognitív képességek (a Cognitive Reflection Test proxijával) erősen korrelálnak a teljesítménnyel; a nem-kognitív mutatók közül mind a türelem, mind a jelenhajlam számítanak, de különböző módon — a jelenhajlam jobban jelzi a vizsgajegyeket, míg a türelem erősebben kapcsolódik a GPA-hoz. Építőkocka-tanulmány annak gondolkodásához, mely preferenciák számítanak az emberi-tőke-formáláshoz.
data_used: Incentivized experimental measures of preferences (risk, time, social, competitive) and cognitive ability (CRT) for ~200 Hungarian university students, linked to exam grades and GPA.
data_used_hu: Ösztönzött kísérleti mérések preferenciákról (kockázat, idő, szociális, versengés) és kognitív képességről (CRT) ~200 magyar egyetemi hallgatón, vizsgajegyekhez és átlagokhoz kapcsolva.
policy_relevance: For Hungarian higher-education policy: the paper's preference measures could be integrated into counselling or career-advice design at universities, and the time-preference results have implications for academic-calendar design (spacing of deadlines, exam windows). Early-stage research; the policy implementation path runs through follow-up work with larger samples and broader populations.
policy_relevance_hu: A magyar felsőoktatási politikához: a tanulmány preferencia-mérései beépíthetők a tanácsadási vagy pályaválasztási tervezésbe az egyetemeken, és az időpreferencia-eredményeknek implikációi vannak az akadémiai naptár-tervezésre (határidők időzítése, vizsgaablakok). Korai szakaszú kutatás; a szakpolitikai megvalósítási út nagyobb mintákon és szélesebb populációkon való utánkövető kutatásokon át vezet.
